items.summary: Implement a multi-level progression system in the 3D fighting game. The game should have at least 3 distinct levels/stages. Each level should increase in difficulty (e.g., more Sukuna enemies, faster/stronger enemies, or more enemies simultaneously). After defeating all enemies in a level, the player advances to the next level with a brief level-transition screen displaying the level number. Completing all levels triggers a final victory screen. The current level number should be displayed in the game HUD alongside the existing health bars.

Add a sword weapon for UG in the 3D fighting game. UG should be able to equip and use a sword via a dedicated key (e.g., 'E' on keyboard). When the sword is active, UG's melee attack range and damage are increased compared to the default punch/kick. The sword should be rendered as a 3D mesh (e.g., a long, thin box or cylinder with an emissive blade material) visibly held in UG's hand. A sword slash visual effect (arc or sweep emissive geometry) should appear during the attack animation. The sword attack should also be triggerable via a new on-screen SWORD button in the OnScreenControls overlay, following the same mousedown/mouseup and touchstart/touchend pattern as existing buttons.

Extend the KeyState and GameState type definitions in frontend/src/types/game.ts to include a 'sword' boolean key for the new sword attack input, a 'currentLevel' number field in game state, and any necessary sword-attack state (e.g., swordActive flag, swordDamageMultiplier) on the Character interface, so the levels and sword systems integrate cleanly with existing TypeScript types.
